feedback.issue: The summary assigns tone :muted to an aborted/canceled pipeline, but the centralized StatusHelper::STATUS_TONES maps "aborted" => :danger (red). Because the design keeps the pipeline's existing status_badge (which colors via STATUS_TONES), the same aborted pipeline could show a gray summary dot and a red status badge simultaneously. ui-style-guide 'Color — Status colors are semantic and reserved — never used decoratively' and 'Core components — one source of truth per component' require a single semantic color per state. Recommend reconciling the tone source: either derive the summary tone from STATUS_TONES, or add an explicit 'canceled' semantic to StatusHelper (a deliberate cancel is arguably muted, not danger) and propose that guide/table addition in the same PR, per CLAUDE.md 'if the guide is silent, follow its principles, then propose a guide addition.'

backend-guide 'Testing' states 'system tests cover the critical flows (pipeline board, gate approval, worker claim API).' The board's live-summary flow is covered with ActionDispatch::IntegrationTest request specs instead of a system test. The spec-writer justified this (no system-test base class configured in the repo), and request specs do exercise load-time true-state, the aria-live region, and the stream subscription, so this is a pragmatic substitution rather than a gap; noted so the deviation from the named testing expectation is explicit and Build can add a system test (or base class) if the board's live in-place update is to be verified end-to-end.
